notes: Flutter renders to a canvas, so the iOS a11y tree is sparse — these bugs are caught by VISION grounding (OmniParser over the screenshot), not the a11y tree. All are visually observable in the counter screen.

bugs.title: Plus increments by 2 instead of 1
bugs.description: Tapping the 'Plus' button increases the displayed count by 2 each press instead of 1.
trigger: From a count of 0, tap 'Plus' once
bugs.expected: The count reads 1 after one Plus.
bugs.actual: The count reads 2 after one Plus (increments by 2).
bugs.expected_finding: Reports that the Plus button increments the counter by 2 (or by the wrong amount) instead of 1.

bugs.title: Reset sets the count to 1 instead of 0
bugs.description: Tapping 'Reset' sets the counter to 1 rather than clearing it to 0.
bugs.expected_finding: Reports that Reset does not clear the counter to 0 (it shows 1).

bugs.title: Subscribe switch label flips but the switch never moves
bugs.description: Tapping the subscribe switch toggles its title text between 'Subscribed' and 'Not subscribed' visually inconsistently while the switch thumb never changes position — the control and its state disagree.
trigger: Compare the switch position to its label
bugs.expected: The switch turns on and the label reads 'Subscribed'.
bugs.actual: The switch stays off (value never changes); the control does not reflect a subscribed state.
bugs.expected_finding: Reports the subscribe toggle/switch does not actually change state when tapped.
